open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — tracked, no server impact (a user can only exhaust their own browser tab). Load boundaries still lack a numPages>1000 / cumulative-byte cap and a processing timeout. Consumers: (1) viewer.js loadDocument() renders one canvas per page (linear; render-race amplifier FIXED+VERIFIED in TASK-316); (2) thumbnails.js renders a SECOND canvas per page (doubles per-page footprint; mitigated by 120px thumb width, dpr cap 2, sequential render, renderToken guard, but page COUNT still unbounded); (3) merge.js validates each file at 50MB but mergeAndDownload() loads base.getData() + every queued file's arrayBuffer() into pdf-lib at once with no cumulative byte/page cap; (4) annotation-summary.js / watermark.js operate only on the already-validated open doc (not new boundaries). Single right fix = developer task: a page-count/byte cap at the viewer.js LOAD boundary protects viewer + thumbnails + every future per-page consumer at once. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

audit_notes.innerHTML_in_js: only empty-string clears (listEl.innerHTML='', layer.innerHTML='') — no user/PDF content sink anywhere in the tree
audit_notes.external_fetch_or_xhr: none (100% client-side, PDFs never leave the browser)
audit_notes.merge_js_filename: buildFileName() strips to [a-zA-Z0-9._-] + '_merged.pdf'; sanitizeName() strips path separators/control chars; displayed filename via textContent only — path-traversal + XSS safe
audit_notes.merge_js_download: Blob(application/pdf) + createObjectURL + a.download + rel=noopener, anchor removed, URL revoked after 1s — safe
audit_notes.merge_js_validation: per-file magic-bytes %PDF- + MIME application/pdf + .pdf extension + empty-file reject + 50MB cap; cumulative cap still absent (SEC-002, tracked)
audit_notes.annotation_summary_js: read-only navigation list; all text via textContent; item.label sourced from annotate.js controlled enum, never user/PDF input; innerHTML only for empty-state clears
audit_notes.secrets_scan_hit: status/security.json only — matched the literal key words in this file; false positive, no real secret
